summary: Tested Arrow Escape backend (products + Stripe checkout) and mobile frontend (home, game, settings, accessibility, store). 4/5 backend tests pass. All frontend flows & testIDs verified: home renders ARROW/ESCAPE title fully, play navigates to game L1 (3x3=9 arrows), solving 0..8 shows modal-won, NEXT goes to L2 (3x4=12 arrows), L50 renders (9x9=81), L100 renders with arrow-100-0 present. Settings & accessibility screens show every required testID. Store calls /api/checkout/session successfully and redirects to Stripe on web. One backend bug: /api/checkout/status/{session_id} returns 500 because Stripe cannot find the just-created session.

critical.issue: Returns 500 CheckoutError('No such checkout.session: cs_test_...'). The session is created successfully by POST /api/checkout/session and a real Stripe test session_id is returned, but the immediate follow-up GET fails. Review request explicitly requires this to return a CheckoutStatusOut (payment_status='unpaid' is acceptable). Suspected cause: emergentintegrations StripeCheckout is re-instantiated with STRIPE_API_KEY for status lookup, but the session was created under a different (routed) key, OR the library/proxy doesn't yet surface retrieve-session for sk_test_emergent. Frontend store screen polls this endpoint after Stripe redirect, so purchases cannot be confirmed end-to-end.

integration_issues.issue: When Stripe redirects back with ?session_id=..., the store screen calls /api/checkout/status/{id} which currently 500s (see backend critical). Poll will eventually give 'Error checking status'. End-to-end purchase grant cannot complete until backend is fixed.

react_native_issues.issue: Deprecation warnings (non-blocking): 'shadow*' → use boxShadow, 'textShadow*' → use textShadow, 'props.pointerEvents' → use style.pointerEvents. These fire on every render. Cosmetic but worth cleaning up.

action_items: Fix GET /api/checkout/status/{session_id}: either (a) read status purely from the MongoDB payment_transactions row and only call Stripe when safe (wrap stripe_checkout.get_checkout_status in try/except and return stored values with payment_status='unpaid' as fallback), or (b) confirm emergentintegrations supports status lookup with sk_test_emergent. Recommendation: wrap the Stripe retrieve call so the endpoint still returns 200 with the persisted row when Stripe retrieval fails (log a warning).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger is referenced inside stripe_webhook (line 215) before it is defined at line 261. In practice Python resolves it at call time so it works, but move logging setup above route definitions for safety.
server.py: checkout_status always calls Stripe even when we only need to surface stored status. Consider short-circuiting with MongoDB-only data if the Stripe call fails, to keep the UX working.
server.py is a single-file 267-line module — acceptable for this size; no split needed yet.
seed_data_creation: One payment_transactions row inserted per test run with session_id from Stripe test mode and status='initiated'. Safe to leave; prefixed user_id='TEST_user'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_arrow_escape.py as regression. All frontend testIDs verified working on mobile viewport (390x844). Stripe redirect works on web (window.location.href). The only unresolved issue is the /api/checkout/status 500.
rca_of_issue: Reproduce: curl -X POST {BACKEND}/api/checkout/session -d '{"product_id":"remove_ads","origin_url":"https://example.com","user_id":"x"}' → 200 with session_id. Then curl {BACKEND}/api/checkout/status/{session_id} → 500. Backend log shows: emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutError: Failed to retrieve session status: No such checkout.session: cs_test_XXX. The session_id IS a valid Stripe test session (URL loads the real Stripe checkout page in browser), but retrieving it via the same StripeCheckout(api_key=sk_test_emergent) client fails. Mitigation: wrap get_checkout_status in try/except; on failure, return CheckoutStatusOut built from the stored Mongo row (status='initiated', payment_status='unpaid', amount_total=int(amount*100), granted=False). This keeps polling from the frontend working and still upgrades to 'paid' once the Stripe webhook (which uses the same library but different code path) updates the row.
